reasoning: Based on FRED data, the 10-year TIPS yield (DFII10) saw a recent sharp spike to 1.95% on August 15, capping a fast 8-basis-point jump over two days despite an overall month-long downward trend. The FRED statistical tool gives a 29% probability that the yield will be higher than 1.95% by August 24, citing strong mean-reversion tendencies and a negative underlying slope (-0.005/day). Furthermore, macro conditions—including a widely expected Federal Reserve rate cut at the September 17 FOMC meeting, following cooler CPI data—broadly support downward pressure on interest rates overall through the fall. Therefore, probabilities suggest a decrease is more likely than an increase across all time horizons, with uncertainty scaling upwards closer to the base rate of 0.5 for dates further into the future.
